title: Frozen benchmark.csv at commit dfa28407
supports: Exact item wording, options, Gold answers and Reference Answers.
limitations: Defines project content but is not independent external scientific evidence.

title: NIST/SEMATECH e-Handbook: Detection of Outliers
supports: Investigate and label potential outliers; delete only when an observation is established as erroneous; otherwise consider robust analysis.
limitations: General statistical guidance; sensor-specific root causes still require domain investigation.

title: Anomaly Detection by Robust Statistics
supports: Robust methods reduce undue influence of anomalous observations and distinguish detection from automatic deletion.
limitations: General methodology, not a sensor-device SOP.

title: NIST/SEMATECH e-Handbook: Response Surface Designs
supports: Factor levels, interactions, curvature, fractional designs and process-window modeling.
limitations: Does not prescribe the exact factors or acceptance thresholds for a paper-tape process.

title: NIST Traceable Reference Material Program for Gas Standards
supports: Calibration gas concentration assignment, certificates, traceability and quality assurance.
limitations: Does not validate a particular MFC, chamber, line-loss or humidity correction.

title: Revision of ISO 19229 to support the certification of calibration gases for purity
supports: Purity analysis, uncertainty and certification of calibration gases.
limitations: Certification framework, not a complete dynamic-dilution validation protocol.

title: NIOSH Pocket Guide: Hydrogen sulfide
supports: Acute toxicity, exposure limits, IDLH, flammability and the unreliability of odor warning.
limitations: Supports no-go and engineering-control boundaries, not experimental dosing instructions.

title: NIOSH Pocket Guide: Chlorine
supports: Low exposure limits, strong oxidizing behavior and serious inhalation/reactivity hazards.
limitations: Supports refusal and containment boundaries, not chlorine generation or dosing instructions.

title: NIOSH: Phosphine
supports: Phosphine toxicity and the need for occupational controls and emergency planning.
limitations: Page access can vary; use current institutional SDS/SOP for site-specific controls.

title: NIOSH 2012-147: Current Strategies for Engineering Controls in Nanomaterial Production and Downstream Handling Processes
supports: Containment, local exhaust, exposure control and safer cleanup for engineered nanomaterials.
limitations: Control selection remains material-, quantity- and process-specific.

title: On the validity of the Arrhenius picture in two-dimensional submonolayer growth
supports: An apparent activation energy can combine multiple processes and should not automatically be assigned to one elementary barrier.
limitations: The physical system differs from gas sensors; supports the evidence boundary, not a sensor-specific mechanism.

title: Oxygen Vacancy Formation Energy in Metal Oxides: High Throughput Computational Studies and Machine Learning Predictions
supports: Vacancy energetics depend on material, oxygen site, cation identity and oxidation state.
limitations: Computed formation energies do not establish vacancy concentration or causality in a measured sample.

title: Predicting core-level X-ray photoemission spectra of oxide surfaces from first principles — a case study for SnO2
supports: SnO2 O 1s spectra can contain overlapping signatures from terminations, vacancies, OH and O2 adsorbates; interpretation is difficult.
limitations: Recent preprint and model surfaces; sample-specific assignments still need orthogonal evidence.

title: Density functional study of oxygen vacancies at the SnO2 surface and subsurface sites
supports: Surface and subsurface oxygen vacancies affect SnO2 electronic structure differently.
limitations: Does not validate a particular O 1s peak deconvolution.

title: XPS on Li-battery-related compounds: analysis of inorganic phases and methodology for charge correction
supports: Absolute binding-energy assignments can be distorted by charging and overlapping phases; additional constraints improve identification.
limitations: Battery materials differ from gas-sensor oxides, but the XPS methodology boundary is transferable.

title: Polyaniline Nanofiber Gas Sensors: Examination of Response Mechanisms
supports: PANI gas sensing involves changes in polymer doping/protonation and conductivity; morphology and diffusion affect response.
limitations: A specific film formulation and environment may behave differently.

title: Fabrication of an ammonia gas sensor using inkjet-printed polyaniline nanoparticles
supports: Printed PANI can provide room-temperature ammonia sensing, supporting route feasibility.
limitations: Does not establish PANI as universally preferable to MOS or MoS2 in wearables.

title: Conductance Model for Single-Crystalline/Compact Metal Oxide Gas Sensing Layers: SnO2(101)
supports: SMOX conductance depends on surface electrostatic potential; n/p carrier type and morphology matter.
limitations: Compact epitaxial layers differ from porous polycrystalline films.

title: Electrical transport and gas sensing in ZnO nanowire networks
supports: Network junctions and contacts can dominate resistance and gas response variability.
limitations: ZnO network results cannot be directly transferred to every nanowire material.

title: Humidity effects on MoS2 gas-sensor response and recovery
supports: Humidity can materially change response and recovery, supporting application-specific humidity validation.
limitations: Specific device architecture and analyte conditions limit generalization.

title: Substrate-dependent ammonia sensing in graphene
supports: Substrate and environment can change gas-sensing response, illustrating context-transfer risk.
limitations: Graphene is an analogy, not direct evidence for every composite route.

title: Capillary flow as the cause of ring stains from dried liquid drops
supports: Pinned-edge evaporation can drive capillary transport and edge accumulation.
limitations: Porous paper also involves infiltration, sorption, chemistry and forced-air drying.

title: Influence of Morphology on the Optical Properties of Metal Nanoparticles
supports: Silver nanoparticle optical response depends strongly on morphology and plasmon modes.
limitations: Does not prove that raising silver-nitrate loading will change a paper from brown to neutral gray.

title: Controlling Silver Nanoparticle Size and Morphology with Photostimulated Synthesis
supports: Illumination, stabilizer and aggregation conditions can alter silver nanoparticle size and morphology.
limitations: Colloidal synthesis differs from silver-nitrate-impregnated paper.

title: Limit of Blank, Limit of Detection and Limit of Quantitation
supports: Detection limits depend on blank behavior, variability and method response rather than highest signal alone.
limitations: The simple 3σ/slope formula is an approximation and method validation must consider errors and model assumptions.

title: Metal oxide decoration on Si-FETs for selective gas sensing at room temperature
supports: CuO-functionalized devices can show strong room-temperature H2S response and recovery.
limitations: Does not independently establish CuO→CuS conversion as the cause of slow recovery; direct phase evidence is still needed.

title: NIST Privacy Framework
supports: Risk-based handling and governance of information disclosure.
limitations: Does not determine which proprietary formulation details this project must redact; that remains project/legal policy.
